items.title: Auto-attach feature implemented in Index 🚀
items.summary: The documentation for the auto-attach feature in the browser-device-bridge has been updated to reflect its successful implementation. Key changes include the introduction of origin-scoped capability grants, allowing for runtime gating without the need for local cookie domains. This update enhances tab management by enabling auto-adoption of same-origin tabs and self-healing on navigation.

items.title: Clean Up API Test Dropdown in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the obsolete `/api/auth/status` option from the Wisdom appliance API test dropdown and its corresponding i18n key across all locales. This change is a follow-up to the removal of the `GET /api/auth/status` endpoint, ensuring that the frontend no longer references a non-existent API. The adjustments enhance the user experience by preventing 404 errors when selecting the outdated option.

items.title: Update logout verification to use /api/status in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Wisdom golden-image runbook to utilize the `/api/status` endpoint for logout verification, replacing the deprecated `/api/auth/status`. The change ensures that the readiness check is streamlined, confirming logout status through the new endpoint without any code path alterations.

items.title: Streamlined Auth Endpoint by Removing Redundant Status Check in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the redundant `GET /api/auth/status` endpoint from the Wisdom backend, consolidating functionality into the existing `GET /api/status` endpoint. Key changes include the deletion of the `login_status()` function and associated tests, along with updates to documentation to reflect this change. The impact is a cleaner API surface and improved efficiency in status checks.

items.title: Enhance Personal WeChat Task Polling with Time Import in Skills ⏰
items.summary: This update imports the standard library time module for improved task polling in Personal WeChat. Additionally, a regression test has been added to ensure successful queued-task completion. The changes have been validated with passing tests and no compilation errors.

items.title: Enhance Icon Alignment in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the layout of various icons across the console pages, ensuring better alignment and visual consistency. Key changes include centering email marketing metrics and deployment card icons, as well as adjustments to wallet address icons. These enhancements contribute to a more polished user interface in the PlatformFrontend.

items.title: Fix untranslated header in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The order-list page now correctly displays the translated header for the operation column by using the existing `order.field.actions` key. This change resolves the issue of showing a raw, untranslated string and ensures consistency across all 12 locales. No new localization keys were added, simplifying the maintenance of translations.

items.title: Enhance K3 with Price-Safe Fallback in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a price-safe fallback for the `kimi-k3` model, allowing it to route requests to available `kimi-k2` models until a live upstream is established. The change ensures that billing is done at the requested `k3` rate, maintaining revenue integrity. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect this new fallback mechanism, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Enhance frictionless attach design in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the design for a zero-manual-attach browser control, detailed in the new document for frictionless attachment. Key features include AI-triggered tab attachment and a robust authorization mechanism based on connector installation. The documentation also outlines the implementation status and highlights the need for owner review before merging.

items.title: Strengthen Origin Validation for Extension Bridge in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the security of the extension's content script by implementing an explicit origin allowlist for the page↔extension cookie bridge. Key changes include the addition of a new file for origin validation and modifications to the message handler to ensure only trusted origins can communicate. The implementation also includes new tests to verify the allowlist behavior and prevent future drift in the manifest.

items.title: Revamp Browser Agent Execution Engine in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new architecture for the Browser Agent, moving away from the previous custom implementation to a more robust and isolated package. Key changes include the integration of Chrome APIs, a refined accessibility hierarchy, and enhanced policy capabilities, which collectively improve reliability and security. The transition aims to streamline operations and ensure a more durable execution environment.

items.title: Exciting New Narrative Blueprints Added in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces new narrative production blueprints for 60-second lyric music videos and milestone documentaries, each featuring 12 narrative cues and a dedicated managed operation. Key enhancements include a cost-aware motion profile and improved API endpoints for blueprint cataloging and project creation. The changes aim to streamline the narrative creation process while ensuring robust validation and error handling.

items.title: Enhance Browser Agent Snapshot Rendering in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the Browser Agent's snapshot rendering capabilities. Key changes include optimized sampling methods, enhanced filtering for hidden elements, and the release of version 0.7.2 of the Browser Agent extension. These enhancements aim to reduce rendering time and improve overall performance, as evidenced by successful validation tests.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Status Mapping for Clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the WeChat status handling into a single, clear mapping, improving the user experience during login. The new structure eliminates confusion by ensuring each backend state is distinctly represented in the UI, with updated labels for various statuses. Additionally, the code has been cleaned up, enhancing maintainability and reducing potential errors.

items.title: Revamp Poivelle UI to Align with Nexior Design in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update integrates Poivelle's UI with Nexior's design tokens, enhancing visual consistency across components. Key changes include updated typography, responsive surfaces, and improved keyboard focus indicators while maintaining existing functionality. The modifications ensure a cohesive user experience without altering any core behaviors.

items.title: Fix account profile sync for WeChat logins in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the console displayed the wrong WeChat account due to improper handling of account switches. The `_persist_prepared_profile` function has been enhanced to ensure that the currently logged-in account's profile is authoritative, replacing stale data when switching accounts. Key tests have been added to verify the correct behavior during account transitions.

items.title: Enhance Hailuo Task States Display in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the display of Hailuo task states by clearly indicating queued, pending, processing, and running statuses. Additionally, it prioritizes failure states for error, failed, and cancelled tasks. The changes include new test cases and modifications to the Preview component for better user feedback.

items.title: Removed distribution risk management button from status page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The distribution risk management button has been successfully removed from the admin distribution status page as requested. This change does not affect the accessibility of the risk management page, which remains reachable via the admin sidebar. The overall functionality and navigation of the console are preserved.

items.title: Fix invoice download flow with authentication in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a 401 error when downloading invoices by ensuring that the download URL is fetched through an authenticated request. The frontend now opens a new tab with the signed URL after successfully retrieving it, enhancing the user experience while maintaining security. Error handling has also been improved to close the tab and display an error message if the download fails.

items.title: Enhance invoice download handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the invoice download functionality to serve signed download URLs as JSON, allowing the SPA to handle the download more effectively. The new `_download_response()` helper function enables the SPA to request the URL with a `?response=json` parameter, while maintaining backward compatibility for direct API callers. This change improves user experience by ensuring authenticated requests can access the download link seamlessly.

items.title: Streamlined Device Management with One Delete Button in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the device management process on the browser devices page by merging the revoke and delete actions into a single delete button. The backend has been adjusted to support a one-step hard delete, enhancing user experience by reducing unnecessary clicks. Additionally, internationalization keys related to the revoked state have been removed to simplify the interface.

items.title: Streamline Device Management with Unified Delete Action in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update merges the revoke and delete actions for browser devices into a single delete operation, simplifying the user experience. The new endpoint now hard-deletes devices and their connections in one step, eliminating the previous revoke requirement. All related tests have been updated and successfully pass, ensuring the integrity and security of the changes.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Login with Auto-dismiss Feature in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an auto-dismiss feature for the WeChat login security notice, enhancing the user experience by removing the blocking notice during login. A new watchdog handler has been implemented and registered, ensuring smooth operation with live verification and four unit tests. Additionally, previous review feedback has been addressed, improving the robustness of the feature.

items.title: Enhance Browser Agent with Semantic Probing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces bound CSS pointer-style probing, separating it from structural DOM scanning to improve reliability. Key changes include caching pointer boundaries and releasing the Browser Agent extension as version 0.7.1. The implementation has passed extensive validation tests, ensuring robust performance in semantic extraction.

items.title: Enhance Product Identity for TVC in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update establishes shot 01 as the canonical reference for the commercial TVC graph, ensuring product identity is preserved across scenes. Key changes include adding typed dependencies for specific shots and implementing checks to prevent reference regeneration when a valid shot is selected. The rollout maintains existing project graphs while introducing new reference edges for future revisions.

items.title: Enhance Heartbeat Management for Local Renders in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of heartbeat leases during synchronous local operations, ensuring that media downloads, rendering, and uploads maintain ownership integrity. Key changes include aborting rendering on heartbeat failures and retrying transient database errors within a confirmed lease window. These enhancements address a production incident where local renders were stuck due to lease conflicts, ensuring smoother operation and reliability.

items.title: Introducing Poivelle: Your New AI Film Workspace in Nexior 🎬
items.summary: This update adds Poivelle as a standalone professional AI film workspace within Nexior, featuring tools like Canvas, Storyboard, Timeline, and Review projections. Key enhancements include a direct API client, mobile/desktop layouts, and comprehensive support for 18 locales. The integration of a LibTV-depth commercial TVC workflow further enriches the production capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Connector Experience with Reconnect Button in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Reconnect' button in the connector detail pane, allowing users to re-authorize connections without needing to disconnect first. The button is only visible for connected rows with a valid catalog, ensuring a seamless user experience. This change improves the workflow for users facing connection issues by eliminating unnecessary steps.

items.title: Streamlined Login Process and UI Updates in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the login experience by optimizing the UI executor during the verification phase, allowing it to release resources sooner. Key changes include a quicker response for the verification code and the introduction of a settle gate to improve the efficiency of the preparation phase. These adjustments lead to a smoother user experience and reduced waiting times.

items.title: Update element-plus to 2.14.3 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the element-plus library from version 2.14.2 to 2.14.3, introducing new features such as virtual scroll support for the transfer component and enabling bundledDev. Additionally, several bug fixes have been implemented, including improvements to the input-otp component's field line-height and form size inheritance. These changes enhance the overall functionality and user experience of the platform.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Connector Browser Policy in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Xiaohongshu Connector browser policy with the merged Skill capability frontmatter. It introduces seven generic BrowserAgent capabilities essential for upstream-MCP parity workflows and locks the 17-capability policy in the connector seed contract test. No changes were made to the core code, ensuring stability while enhancing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Suno Post-Deploy Evidence in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive post-deploy section to the Suno JSON parity report, including authoritative build data and archived production JSON for retesting. Key improvements include distinguishing current results from historical snapshots and ensuring all archived JSON parses correctly. The final verified state shows high alignment across various audio actions and helper functions, with a few remaining gaps noted for future attention.

items.title: Enhance X9 Finalize Proof Foundation in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the X9 finalize proof foundation, enabling local quote generation and validation within the X402Backend. Key changes include the removal of dependencies on PlatformGateway, the implementation of RFC 8785 for proof verification, and the introduction of a new pricing module. These enhancements aim to improve the reliability and security of payment processing in the system.

items.title: Mirror Xiaohongshu MCP Workflows in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update benchmarks Xiaohongshu business workflows against the upstream repository, ensuring all routes, state machines, and interactions are preserved in the Skill layer. Key features include enhanced login checks, deterministic publish success, and comprehensive interaction state validations. The addition of a new MCP parity reference solidifies the alignment with upstream functionalities.

items.title: Enhance MIDI Conversion Process in Platform Service 🎹
items.summary: This update implements a sequential approach for the MountSea MIDI conversion, ensuring all-or-nothing completion and durable checkpoints. It removes the previous three-way concurrency that led to account capacity issues, resulting in a more reliable processing method. The changes have been validated with successful tests and improved performance metrics.

items.title: Enhance X identity and read capabilities in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the `whoami` functionality through authenticated X account settings and introduces strict verification for screen names. It also improves the handling of Cloudflare errors and adds regression tests to ensure reliability. Key changes include enhanced filtering for UserMedia and updates to the X skill playbook.

items.title: Introducing Fail-Closed Bazaar Discovery in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update lays the groundwork for the X8 Bazaar discovery feature, which is currently set to default-off. Key enhancements include the integration of route declarations using official Bazaar extension helpers, validation of signed-catalog schemas, and a robust payment lifecycle requirement. The changes also ensure strict security measures for credential handling and URL policies, while maintaining a focus on testing and validation.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from Chinese Translations in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automatic translation updates for internationalization (i18n) files based on changes from the Chinese (zh-CN) source. Key modifications include the addition of new status messages across multiple languages, enhancing user experience and accessibility. The changes affect Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese translations.

items.title: Enhance MIDI Aggregation for Non-Vocal Stems in Platform Service 🎶
items.summary: This update introduces a robust method for converting unique non-vocal stems into MIDI using MountSea. It aggregates non-empty instrument tracks into the TTAPI public MIDI contract while ensuring deduplication and complete success for each conversion stage. The implementation processes MDI conversions in durable batches, enhancing reliability and performance.

items.title: Enhance Wisdom Runtime Status UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update classifies all Wisdom runtime statuses into distinct categories such as loading, verification, and failure. It also restricts QR rendering to states supported by the appliance QR endpoint and adds a prebuild contract test for the `/api/status` flow. These changes improve the user experience and ensure better synchronization between the UI and backend states.

items.title: Streamline BrowserAgent Modes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes unsupported snapshot options from the V1 model schema, including depth and interactive modes. It ensures that only executable observation tools are available, preventing errors in snapshot generation. Validation tests confirm that the changes maintain functionality and improve reliability.

items.title: Revamped Earning Documentation for Clarity and Engagement in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update overhauls the 'Earning Alliance' documentation, ensuring accuracy and user-friendliness. Key changes include fact corrections, removal of misleading content, and the addition of hands-on tutorials with visual aids. The new structure aims to empower users with clear, actionable insights while maintaining alignment with platform code.

items.title: Enhance Console Spacing for a Cleaner Look in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update standardizes the spacing across multiple console pages, particularly addressing the inconsistent margins in KPI card layouts. Key changes include the introduction of a global row gap for card rows and the removal of redundant padding in the Console layout, improving the overall visual consistency and usability. The adjustments affect 11 files, ensuring a cohesive design across the platform.

items.title: Expanded Agent Client Variants in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces additional Agent client variants, including Amp, Cline, and VS Code user MCP variants. The compiled Fish manifest has been expanded from 6 to 9 Agent clients and from 21 to 25 variants, enhancing visibility and functionality. Key adjustments include refining verification statuses and aligning expiration times.

items.title: Launch of BrowserAgent V1 Extension 0.7.0 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The BrowserAgent V1 extension has been released as version 0.7.0, aligning package, lockfile, and Chrome manifest versions without any runtime code changes. Validation checks confirmed zero vulnerabilities and all tests passed successfully, ensuring a smooth upgrade for users. Existing installations need to reload and pair again to record the final capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Skill with BrowserAgent V1 in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Xiaohongshu skill with the final BrowserAgent V1 tools by replacing outdated references and improving media validation processes. Key changes include the removal of local approval language and the introduction of opaque resource IDs for media uploads. The rollout enhances user experience by reducing redundant consent prompts during browser selection.

items.title: Enhance Settlement Reconciliation with X5 Provider Support in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the X5 provider-pinned settlement reconciler, enhancing the payment processing capabilities. Key features include automated sweeping of pending payments, validation checks before accepting transactions, and a new CLI for sweeping and repairing payments. The changes aim to improve reliability and efficiency in settlement processes while ensuring production safety until further validation is completed.

items.title: Enhance Evidence Runners for Agent Clients in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces fixed-version native evidence runners for Amp, Cline, and VS Code, ensuring secure installation with SHA-256 verification. It also allows nonsecret client evidence on branch workflow dispatch while maintaining restrictions on protected hosted MCP discovery. Comprehensive cross-platform configurations and regression coverage have been added to enhance reliability.

items.title: Introducing Durable Payment State and Artifacts in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update establishes the X4 durable payment-state and artifact framework, enhancing the existing X3 infrastructure. Key features include the creation of a dedicated `x402_payments` ledger, robust state management, and secure artifact storage with AES-256-GCM encryption. The implementation ensures unique payment identities and supports retry mechanisms, while production capabilities remain disabled until necessary adapters are available.

items.title: Exciting New X3 Resource Server Features in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the official X3 resource server lifecycle for the X402 backend, enhancing the SERP allowlist functionality. Key changes include binding signed catalog snapshots to requests, implementing a pluggable Facilitator client, and enforcing strict limits on request and response handling. The rollout is currently in draft mode, pending the implementation of durable payment artifacts.

items.title: Streamline Login State with Wisdom Runtime in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the `WisdomRuntimeState` to align with the appliance's API status contract, removing redundant polling requests. It simplifies the rendering of security verification and QR/login transitions by utilizing a single source for runtime status. Additionally, it ensures that the authoritative status is refetched after a rejected verification code, improving the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Login Status Visibility in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes detected logged-out WeChat pages through the `/api/status.status` endpoint, improving clarity by removing the ambiguous `waiting_for_login` state. The product lifecycle states `preparing`, `ready`, and `error` remain intact, while the web login flow and documentation have been updated to reflect these changes. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the integrity of the new status mappings.

items.title: Transforming Timelines into MP4s in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a robust FFmpeg H.264/AAC MP4 renderer, replacing the previous JSON placeholder. Key enhancements include freezing confirmed timelines into the ExecutionPlan, validating media properties, and normalizing error handling. The deployment process has been refined to ensure seamless transitions and rollback capabilities.

items.title: Implement X7 Provider Calibration Release Gate in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the fail-closed X7 provider calibration release gate, enhancing validation processes by requiring a truthful Ace raw baseline and multiple independent third-party reports. Key additions include new provider configurations, a protected-CI HMAC attestation, and a deterministic canonical bundle digest. The PR also includes extensive tests to ensure compliance and security, paving the way for future live calibration capabilities.

items.title: Exciting New X402 Conformance Runner Features in X402 Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a provider-neutral raw X402 v2 conformance runner, which includes 53 core-v2 cases with deterministic EIP-3009 vectors. It generates JSON, Markdown, and fail-closed JUnit reports while categorizing errors into protocol, infrastructure, profile, ambiguity, and runner errors. The changes enhance the testing framework without impacting production traffic.

items.title: Enhanced Retry Logic for MountSea Painting Failures in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a robust retry mechanism for terminal MountSea painting failures, allowing up to three attempts while maintaining distinct durable stages for each try. The changes ensure that only specific errors are retried, improving reliability without impacting copyright or moderation processes. Validation tests confirm the implementation's effectiveness and safety.

items.title: Enhance MountSea Audio Processing in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update preserves MountSea account affinity by directly passing cropped clips to the `add_vocals` and `add_instrumental` functions, eliminating the need for intermediate re-uploads. Additionally, a contract guard has been added to prevent issues during the painting-specific upload stage. The changes improve the reliability of audio processing and billing for users.

items.title: Align TTAPI Suno Helper Responses in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the MountSea `/suno/mashup-lyrics` route following a change in the TTAPI production response from 404 to 200. It aligns the Mashup Lyrics output with the current TTAPI data contract, ensuring consistency in fields like `status`, `tags`, `text`, and `title`. Additionally, it retains `is_public` and `source_audio_url` in the Persona List items to match the latest TTAPI specifications.

items.title: Enhance Invoice Status Notifications in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces reliable notifications for issued, rejected, and voided invoices using Tencent SES service templates. It ensures notifications are localized by invoice region and sent only after transaction commits, improving user experience and tracking. Additionally, it implements strict validation for Stripe webhook signatures and enhances the testing suite with new database-free tests.

items.title: Enhance Invoice Workflow and Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the admin invoice table by implementing the correct backend pagination and displaying all invoice applications by default. Key changes include clarifying invoice states, enhancing the layout for better mobile behavior, and adding focused UI regression tests. Overall, these enhancements aim to streamline the invoice management process and improve user experience.

items.title: Launch Complete BrowserAgent V1 Tool Set in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the full 26-tool BrowserAgent launch catalog on protocol V1, enhancing the authorization capabilities during pairing. Key changes include the addition of bounded observation and deterministic interaction, while unsupported local modes are now explicitly fail-closed. The validation process has successfully passed all tests, ensuring a robust deployment.

items.title: Launch Complete BrowserAgent V1 Tool Registry in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update defines the complete 26-tool BrowserAgent launch registry directly on protocol V1. Key changes include replacing pre-launch aliases with final tool names, publishing strict JSON schemas for multi-mode browser calls, and enforcing these schemas server-side. This is the first V1-only launch PR, and it should be merged with the corresponding AuthBackend and PlatformFrontend implementations.

items.title: Finalize BrowserAgent V1 Capabilities in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update defines the final capabilities for BrowserAgent V1, mapping 26 tools to 25 authorization capabilities. It removes outdated labels and validates the pairing capability advertisements against the final V1 allowlist. Additionally, the Xiaohongshu browser connector policy has been updated to reflect these final capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Invoice Status Display in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the presentation of invoice statuses on order details by separating the invoice label from its status and actions using a responsive layout. Ineligible orders are now displayed as a subdued informational callout with an icon, while existing behaviors for invoice application, cancellation, status, and download remain intact. The changes ensure a cleaner and more user-friendly interface.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Security Verification Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new WeChat security verification form, allowing users to enter a 4–8 digit code during the login process. Key changes include improved polling for verification status and handling of different outcomes such as rejected and pending codes. The implementation also includes multilingual support with updated text for various languages.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Verification Flow in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the WeChat verification process by allowing polling through transitional states like `logging_in` and `login_other`. The timeout for verification outcomes has been extended from 30 seconds to 120 seconds, ensuring a smoother user experience. Key tests have been added to validate the new flow, and all existing tests have passed successfully.

items.title: Ensure Stable MountSea Suno Deployment in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update maintains the MountSea Suno deployment at one replica, applying the existing deployment and service configurations. Key changes include updates to the deployment contract test to prevent unintended scaling down of replicas. The production environment is now stable with the latest image and zero restarts.

items.title: Enhance MountSea Suno Responses for TTAPI Compatibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns MountSea Suno responses with TTAPI field allow-lists across various routes, including Audios and Vox. Key changes include reproducing TTAPI Vox range types and an 11-level waveform contract, while also preparing MIDI stems for MountSea MDI. The `/suno/mashup-lyrics` endpoint remains unregistered due to a native 404 response from TTAPI.

items.title: Enhance Media Reuse for Efficient Workflows in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to media artifact handling by allowing the reuse of selected media takes across executions. Key changes include the idempotent creation of media takes, enhanced visibility of committed artifacts, and refined selection processes to prevent unnecessary regeneration of already approved dependencies. These enhancements streamline workflows and optimize resource usage in commercial TVC validations.

items.title: Enhance Agent Guide Markdown Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes optional platform placeholders from the shared MCP Markdown, ensuring clarity in the Agent guides. It preserves app labels for both project and user variants and introduces a compiled-project regression test for app context and platform-independent Markdown. The changes enhance the user experience by providing clearer instructions without unnecessary platform references.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Security Verification Outcomes in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies the WeChat security verification process by maintaining the existing flow while explicitly reporting outcomes as 'logged_in', 'rejected', or 'pending'. New tests have been added to validate these outcomes, ensuring robust functionality. The changes improve user experience by eliminating ambiguity in verification status.

items.title: Enhance Group Member Resolution with Invite Capability in Wisdom 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces invite-aware contact resolution, ensuring that only contacts with the ability to invite are considered when creating groups. The changes include modifications to the group creation process and the addition of tests to validate the new functionality. Overall, this enhancement improves the user experience by reducing ambiguity in contact resolution.

items.title: Enhance Message Sending with Idempotency and Retry Logic in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces robust retry mechanisms for transient clipboard failures and adds an optional idempotency key for message sends. The changes ensure that duplicate submissions return the original task, enhancing user experience and reliability. Additionally, the implementation includes strict idempotency checks to prevent unintended duplicates after uncertain UI interactions.

items.title: Enhanced Kling Video Execution Calibration in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update improves the Kling video execution by accurately parsing top-level media URL fields and switching the default model to the production-validated 'kling-v3'. It also includes new regression tests to ensure compatibility with the updated response structure. The changes enhance the reliability of video generation tasks, as evidenced by successful task completions.

items.title: Enhance task polling for Kling and Suno in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the task polling mechanism for Kling and Suno to use the documented `id` field instead of `task_id`. It preserves the existing Seedream `id` contract and includes a fallback for empty responses. The changes ensure that Poivelle accurately records task states, improving reliability in task management.

items.title: Clean Up Connection JSON Residue in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes unnecessary fields from the Connection metadata, specifically `browser_origins` and `required_capabilities`, streamlining the data structure. It also eliminates migration-only markers while preserving historical connection rows. The changes enhance the API's response efficiency and maintain essential OAuth metadata integrity.

items.title: Enhance MountSea Suno JSON Parity with Comprehensive Evidence in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a complete set of paired JSON data for MountSea Suno, including 32 detailed comparison tables and full request/response validation. Key additions include comprehensive action coverage for `/suno/audios`, along with strict OpenAPI validation and machine-readable evidence files. The findings highlight significant differences in audio handling and task retrieval between MountSea and TTAPI.

items.title: Publish verified Agent client variants in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces 21 evidence-backed MCP variants across six Agent clients, enhancing project/user scope and platform selectors for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The changes include a new Fish compiler regression for six apps and the verification of 35 variants, all passing the freshness gate. Pending clients like Cursor and VS Code will be addressed once equivalent evidence is available.

items.title: Enhance Provider Polling SLA Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of provider tasks in the reconciliation process by ensuring that tasks with known IDs are retained until their configured timeout. It also maintains a three-attempt limit for idempotent redispatches when no task ID is returned. Comprehensive tests confirm the stability and effectiveness of these changes.

items.title: Streamline Connection Metadata by Removing Legacy Auth Method in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the redundant `Connection.metadata.auth_method` key from existing rows using an idempotent PostgreSQL JSONB migration. The legacy key is defensively hidden in the `ConnectionSerializer` during the rollout, ensuring that nested OAuth protocol metadata remains intact. The migration has been validated with comprehensive tests, confirming its effectiveness without altering critical connection data.

items.title: Strengthen BrowserAgent Functions in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the security of active BrowserAgent functions, including click, key, and screenshot capabilities. Key changes include blocking sensitive labels and cross-origin interactions, while refining key support to only allow `Enter` and `Escape`. The improvements ensure that page snapshots maintain their integrity and that screenshot captures are accurately tied to the active tab.

items.title: Enhance admin order creation with improved locking in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the admin order creation process by restricting the `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock to the Application row, ensuring that the nullable Service relation is eagerly loaded without unnecessary locks. A new PostgreSQL regression test has been added to validate the functionality with a Usage Application where the service is NULL. Overall, this fix addresses a critical HTTP 500 error encountered in production, enhancing stability and reliability.

items.title: Exciting New Commercial TVC Workflow Added in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the `commercial.tvc@1` Project schema along with a 40-second PulseCam X1 commercial blueprint featuring 10 shots across 6 acts. New APIs for blueprint creation and storyboard projection have been implemented, enhancing the Project Graph capabilities. Additionally, managed execution is now secured with superuser checks and an AceDataCloud API token, ensuring robust cost management and reconciliation.

items.title: Fix run state and deployment issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues in the Poivelle deployment process and execution run state. Key changes include the addition of a missing deployment script, improved error handling for deployment failures, and enhancements to workspace membership discovery for JWT actors. The changes have been validated with comprehensive tests, ensuring stability and functionality.

items.title: Enhance UTF-8 Decoding for Agent Clients in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update implements explicit UTF-8 decoding for client stdout and stderr across all runners, preventing crashes from malformed bytes. Key improvements include replacing invalid bytes and preserving ASCII assertions, ensuring robust output handling. The changes have been validated with 54 passing tests and a clean Ruff check.

items.title: Enhance Agent Evidence Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the execution of npm shims on Windows by utilizing isolated cmd.exe. It also marks Codex project fixtures as trusted before testing and documents the trust prerequisite. Key validation steps were completed, including passing 53 Agent tests and ensuring proper configuration parsing.

items.title: Revalidate MountSea Suno Parity in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update focuses on the revalidation of the full 13-route public Suno surface following the MountSea production rollout. Key changes include successful comparisons of the Sea channel with the default provider across various levels, and documentation of all 17 `/suno/audios` actions with production task evidence. The Voice Persona workflow has been completed, although there is a current limitation with persona-song capacity.

items.title: Enhance Voice Persona Affinity Handling in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update preserves the `voicePersona/complete.vocal_clip_id` as private account-affinity metadata, ensuring that the same-account clip is utilized for VOX persona playlist creation. It introduces fail-closed mechanisms for legacy personas and missing playlist IDs while maintaining the existing public response schemas. Comprehensive tests confirm the stability and reliability of the changes.

items.title: Complete Whitelabel Earning Manual with Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This PR expands the 'Earning' section by adding 24 new Chinese documents, creating a comprehensive manual on whitelabel strategies, referral mechanics, and API resale. Key additions include practical guides for using connectors across various platforms like Bilibili, CSDN, and LinkedIn, enhancing content marketing capabilities for developers. The updates ensure accurate representation of platform functionalities and provide clear guidelines for users.

items.title: Restoring Deploy Permissions for Smooth Builds in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the `contents: write` permission for the build-number action, ensuring that the deployment process can create the next build-number ref without encountering HTTP 403 errors. The serialized deployment concurrency and other scoped permissions remain intact, allowing for efficient and secure deployments.

items.title: Enhance Document Navigation with Expand-All Feature in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an opt-in expand-all mode for the shared document tree, specifically for the earning and promotion category. The legacy sidebar and mobile drawer remain consistent while preserving the collapsed behavior for development and application documents. Key validations have been performed to ensure functionality and maintain existing features.

items.title: Streamlined Admin Order Creation Dialog in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new order creation dialog for admins, enhancing the order management experience. Key features include application validation, dynamic package selection, and a custom pricing option with real-time credit guidance. The dialog is designed to be responsive and user-friendly, ensuring a smooth workflow for order creation.

items.title: Launch admin-only order creation API in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated API for admin-only order creation, enhancing the order management capabilities. Key features include a new context for admin orders, support for custom package creation, and robust protections against unauthorized modifications. The implementation ensures that orders are tied to the customer application, maintaining integrity and security throughout the process.

items.title: Enhance backend deployment stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several key improvements to the backend deployment process. It serializes production workflows to prevent stale deployments, implements a fail-fast mechanism for rendering errors, and adds retry logic for transient failures. These changes aim to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the deployment pipeline.

items.title: Streamline Connector Logic by Removing Legacy Contracts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the connector logic by eliminating legacy runtime contracts, specifically removing compatibility fields for `auth_methods`, `auth_mode`, and `kind`. It introduces a canonical approach for dispatching behaviors across various connection types and updates internal APIs and serializers accordingly. The changes enhance maintainability and ensure that only relevant fields are retained, paving the way for a cleaner integration with the AuthFrontend and PlatformService.

items.title: Streamline Connector Contracts in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the aichat2 connector contracts by removing legacy fallbacks and enforcing a required `connector_identifier` in the AuthBackend connection contract. Key changes include the consumption of canonical execution types and credential types, enhancing the overall clarity and reliability of the connection handling process. The PR has passed all tests, ensuring stability and functionality.

items.title: Enhance billing attribution with self-reporting channel in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a self-reporting mechanism for the `channel=ali` in the billing attribution process. The changes ensure that the `channel` is injected into the `/record` and `/price` payloads, allowing accurate usage tracking without leaking client information. The implementation is configurable via the `HAPPYHORSE_CHANNEL` environment variable, defaulting to `ali`.

items.title: Streamline Connector Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes legacy frontend contract elements, directly consuming connection methods and credentials. Key compatibility types have been eliminated, simplifying the codebase while maintaining essential flows. The changes enhance clarity and performance in the connector browsing and installation processes.

items.title: Enhance conversation search stability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a 2-second wait for the Qt search popover to stabilize before interaction. It requires four consecutive stable samples of the same UIA cell and includes runtime ID and rectangle in the stability signature. The changes ensure a more reliable user experience by validating the live cell immediately before clicking and maintaining fail-closed paths for any failures.

items.title: Boost Appliance Proxy Timeout for Better Performance in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the appliance proxy read timeout from 12 seconds to 120 seconds for UIA-backed endpoints, ensuring smoother operations. Additionally, the Gunicorn worker timeout has been raised from 120 seconds to 150 seconds to maintain authoritative deadlines. The changes also include mapping appliance read timeouts to HTTP 504 errors instead of the generic 502, enhancing error handling.

items.title: Enhance WCDB Logging for Better Insights in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive logging for the WCDB preparation stages, including parent RPC timing and worker operation lifecycles. Key processes such as database discovery and warmup stages are now logged, while maintaining a controlled log volume by adjusting log levels. The changes ensure no timeout or preparation behavior is altered, enhancing monitoring capabilities without impacting performance.

items.title: Introducing Claude Fable 5 to the Chat Models in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds Claude Fable 5 as the first option in the Claude model picker, enhancing our chat capabilities with advanced reasoning, vision, and file handling features. Localization has been improved with new translations for 17 languages, ensuring a broader reach. All changes have been validated with focused tests and code quality checks.

items.title: Introduce Claude Fable 5 to AiChat Models in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `claude-fable-5` model to the AiChat v2 public model list and includes a new model contract test to ensure its functionality. Validation has confirmed that all tests, including focused contract tests, passed successfully after an ordering fix. The merge is contingent on the visibility of PlatformService Fable, with the necessary Mongo route already live.

items.title: Introducing Claude Fable 5 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: Claude Fable 5 has been added to the public model catalog as the flagship model, enhancing our offerings with its 1M context and long-horizon capabilities. The canonical introduction has been updated to reflect this change, ensuring users are informed about its advanced features. All relevant tests have passed, confirming the stability and readiness of this new model.

items.title: Expose Claude Fable 5 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `claude-fable-5` to the OpenAI-compatible models list at `/v1/models`, enhancing our AI capabilities. The routing remains controlled by the Mongo checker, ensuring stability and performance. All routing tests have passed successfully, confirming the integration's reliability.

items.title: Ensured Aichat and Kimi CLIs are Aligned with Documentation in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update confirms that both Aichat and Kimi CLIs are fully synchronized with the latest documentation changes. Key findings include the reduction of the `reasoning_effort` enum in Kimi and the addition of `kimi-k2.6` in Aichat's model list, both of which are already reflected in the CLI code. No code changes were necessary as all elements were verified to match exactly.

items.title: Enhance Agent Catalog with New Integrations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates real application assets for Windows, macOS, and Linux into the Agent catalog, alongside new Markdown guides and project/user/platform recipe variants. It introduces pending integrations for Qwen Code, Cline, and Amp across 20 services while ensuring client templates are validated in isolated profiles to maintain security. The changes enhance the overall functionality and usability of the platform.

items.title: Revamp Grok Fast Video Pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the billing structure for the `grok-imagine-video-1.5-fast` service, aligning it with TTAPI's new quota tiers of 6/15/25. The pricing now reflects charges of 0.63, 1.575, and 2.625 Credits for durations of 1-10s, 11-20s, and 21-30s respectively. Additionally, regression tests have been added to ensure coverage for omitted durations and transitions between tiers.

items.title: Enhance Agent Guide Workbench Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Agent guide workbench by rendering backend-provided selector icons with reliable fallbacks and presenting Agent prose as free-form Markdown. Additionally, code headers have been compacted, and long localized text is now better handled on mobile devices. These changes enhance usability and accessibility for users.

items.title: Enhance Fish Model ID Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of Fish model response IDs by accepting the live `_id` for verification voice model creation while maintaining compatibility with legacy response shapes. It also ensures that empty or non-string identifiers are rejected, streamlining the MountSea voice workflow. Comprehensive tests confirm that the changes are functioning correctly and do not introduce errors.

items.title: Introducing Poivelle: A New Creative Studio Backend in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update adds Poivelle, a standalone FastAPI and MongoDB backend designed for professional creative studios. Key features include a comprehensive Project Graph, enhanced authentication with JWTs, and a robust deployment setup with Docker and Kubernetes. The implementation ensures high availability and security for studio clients while streamlining project management and execution workflows.

items.title: Complete BrowserAgent 1.0 Capabilities in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the comprehensive BrowserAgent 1.0 capability-completion RFC, defining 22 core tools and 4 optional observation tools. It establishes a new protocol version, ensuring all workflows remain Skill-only while enhancing security and functionality. The changes aim to streamline browser interactions and improve user experience by consolidating capabilities and maintaining compatibility with previous versions.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Request Cloaking in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update explicitly enables CLIProxyAPI's `auto` cloaking for the Claude setup-token authentication file, ensuring that requests from non-Claude-Code clients receive the necessary billing headers. Additionally, it documents third-party system prompt handling, token rotation, and upstream validation processes. The changes improve the overall functionality and reliability of the OAuth path, addressing previous issues that led to production errors.

items.title: Upgrade @unhead/vue to 3.1.8 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @unhead/vue package from version 3.1.6 to 3.1.8, introducing several bug fixes and performance improvements. Key changes include optimizations for SSR hot paths, reduced bundle size, and enhancements to the handling of script callbacks across frameworks. These updates aim to improve the overall efficiency and reliability of the application.
